CLAIM.description: Displays the name of the claim.

OPERATION.description:
Specifies how the claim is used. Operation include: EQUALS, HAS
MEMBER, SET APPLICATION USER.

VALUE.description: Display the expected value of the claim in a JWT token.

JWT_USER_MAPPINGS.description: Lists all of the user-JWT mappings configured in the SAP HANA database.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the SAP HANA user name.

JWT_PROVIDER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the JWT provider.

EXTERNAL_IDENTITY.description: Displays the name of the user known to the JWT provider.

KEY_MANAGEMENT_CONFIGURATIONS.description: Displays information about the existing key management configurations.

CONFIGURATION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the key management configuration.

IS_ACTIVE.description:
Indicates whether this configuration is used by LSS to define access to SAP HANA's
sensitive information.

TYPE.description: Displays the type of external key management.

CLIENT_VERSION.description: Displays the version of the driver software.

PROPERTIES.description:
Displays a reduced version of the properties section of the<settings>JSON (the "credentials" object is
omitted).

CONFIGURATION_ID.description:
Displays the ID that uniquely identifies a key management
configuration.

LCM_PRODUCTS.description: Provides information about all installed product versions.

VENDOR_NAME.description: Dispalys the vendor name, for example, sap.com.

PRODUCT_NAME.description: Displays the technical name of the product.

VERSION.description: Displays the version number of the PPMS product version, for example, 1.0.

PPMS_ID.description:
Displays the technical ID of the product version, for example, the version one
identifying it in PPMS.

PRODUCT_DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the human-readable name of the product.

SP_STACK_ID.description: Displays the SP stack ID of the PPMS product version.

SP_STACK_DESCRIPTION.description: Displasy the human-readable name or description of the SP stack.

LCM_PRODUCT_INSTANCES.description: Provides information about all installed product instances.

VENDOR_NAME.description: Displays the name of the vendor. For example, sap.com.

PRODUCT_NAME.description: Displays the technical name of the product.

INSTANCE_DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the human-readable name of the product instance.

LCM_PRODUCT_INSTANCES_INCLUDED.description: Provides information about all installed included product instances.

VENDOR_NAME.description: Displays the name of the vendor. For example, sap.com.

PRODUCT_NAME.description: Displays the technical name of the product.

INSTANCE_DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the human-readable name of the product instance.

MASTER_PRODUCT_NAME.description: Displays the technical name of the including product.

MASTER_INSTANCE_ID.description: Displays the instance key of the including product instance.

LCM_SOFTWARE_COMPONENTS.description: Provides all installed software component versions.

VENDOR_NAME.description: Displays the name of the vendor, for example, sap.com.

COMPONENT_NAME.description: Displays the technical name of the software component version.

VERSION.description: Displays the version of the delivery unit (=SWC), for example, 1.

VERSION_SP.description: Displays the number of the SP, for example, 3.

VERSION_PATCH.description: Displays the number of the patch, for example, 37.

PPMS_ID.description:
Displays the technical ID of the software component version, for example, the one
identifying it in PPMS.

COMPONENT_DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the human-readable name of the software component version.

SP_NAME.description:
Displays the technical name of the support package, for example, SAPK-10000INATEST2 or
SP000.

SP_DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the human-readable name of the support package.

SP_PPMS_ID.description: Displays the 20-digit PPMS number of the installed support package.

LCM_SWID.description: Provides all software ID tags for installed software.

ENTITLEMENT_REQUIRED.description:
Displays whether the SWID for the PV is available with tag entitlementDataRequired:
TRUE/FALSE (default).

PRODUCT_NAME.description: Displays the link to technical name of the product (LCM_PRODUCTS).

LDAP_PROVIDER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the LDAP provider.

LDAP_PROVIDER_ID.description: Displays the ID of the LDAP provider.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time.

IS_DEFAULT.description: Displays whether the the LDAP provider is the default provider: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_SSL_USED.description:
Displays whether the connection to the configured LDAP Server uses SSL: TRUE/FALSE. If
SSL ON is specified for the provider, or if USER LOOKUP URL or
NESTED GROUP LOOKUP URL use 'ldaps://', the value is TRUE.
Otherwise, the value is FALSE.

IS_PROVIDER_ENABLED.description: Displays whether the LDAP provider is enabled for use: TRUE/FALSE.

ATTRIBUTE_DN.description: Displays the LDAP attribute to provide the LDAP distinguished name of a user.

ATTRIBUTE_MEMBER_OF.description:
Displays the LDAP attribute that provides the list of LDAP groups that the user is a
member of.

USER_TYPE_FOR_LDAP_USER_CREATION.description:
Displays the type of user to create when LDAP user creation is enabled. NULL if LDAP user
creation is not enabled: STANDARD, RESTRICTED, NULL

LDAP_PROVIDER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the LDAP provider.

URL TYPE.description: Displays the LDAP provider URL type.

URL.description: Displays the URL for the LDAP provider.

LDAP_USERS.description: Shows information about the users using LDAP authorization.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user.

DN.description: Displays the distinguished name of the user in the LDAP server.

LAST_AUTHORIZATION_REFRESH_TIME.description: Displays the time of the last successful LDAP authorization refresh.

LIBRARIES.description: Provides information about available public language libraries.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the library.

LIBRARY_NAME.description: Displays the name of the library.

LIBRARY_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the library.

DEFAULT_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the unqualified objects in the library.

DEFINITION.description: Displays the query string for the library.

LIBRARY_TYPE.description: Displays the language of the library: SQLSCRIPT, BUILTIN.

IS_VALID.description:
Displays whether the library is valid: TRUE when base objects are valid and the
definition is valid. FALSE when the base objects for the library
are changed or dropped.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time for the library.

LIBRARY_MEMBERS.description: Provides member information for SQLScript user-defined libraries.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the library.

LIBRARY_NAME.description: Displays the name of the library.

LIBRARY_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the library.

MEMBER_TYPE.description: Displays the member type: VARIABLE, PROCEDURE, or FUNCTION.

ACCESS_MODE.description:
Displays the access mode of the library member:PUBLIC when the member is accessible without
restriction.PRIVATE when the member is accessible within the
library.

DEFINITION.description: Displays the definition of the library member.

PRAGMAS.description: Displays the SQLScript user-defined test library pragmas.

COMMENTS.description: Displays the comments defined on user-defined libraries and members.

OBJECT_CATEGORY.description: Displays the object category of the object. The default is NULL.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the object.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the object type.

OBJECT_DEPENDENCIES.description: Provides information about dependencies between objects, such as which views refer to a specific table.

BASE_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the database name of the base objects.

BASE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the base object.

BASE_OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name of the base object.

BASE_OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the base object.

DEPENDENT_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the database name of the dependent object.

DEPENDENT_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the dependent object.

DEPENDENT_OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name of the dependent object.

DEPENDENT_OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the base dependency.

DEPENDENCY_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of dependency between the base and dependent
objects:Direct dependency (for example, the view uses a table)Indirect dependency that is referenced via direct dependency (for
example, the view references a table via another view)SAP internal (dependency to an implicit created object in
SQLScript)SAP internal (dependency to an implicit created object in
SQLScript)Dependency via a referential constraint

OBJECT_PRIVILEGES.description: Provides information about the types of objects and privileges that can be granted to those types of objects.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the object type.

PRIVILEGE.description:
Displays the privilege that can be granted to this type of
object.

OWNERSHIP.description: Provides owner information for the objects available to the user who is querying the view.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the object.

OWNER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the object owner.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the object type.

APPLICATION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the application used for object creation.

PARTITIONED_TABLES.description: Provides general partitioning information for all partitions of a table.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

LEVEL_1_EXPRESSION.description:
Displays the partitioning expression for first-level
partitions.

LEVEL_1_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of partitions at the first level.

LEVEL_1_TYPE.description:
Displays the partitioning type at the first level: HASH, RANGE,
ROUNDROBIN, REPLICATE, or RANGE HETEROGENEOUS.

LEVEL_2_EXPRESSION.description: Displays the partitioning expression for second-level partitions.

LEVEL_2_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of partitions at the second level.

LEVEL_2_TYPE.description:
Displays the partitioning type at the second level: HASH, RANGE,
RANGE HETEROGENEOUS, or HASH HETEROGENEOUS. Heterogeneous
indicates an unbalanced partitioning scheme.

CROSS_STORAGE_UNIQUE_CONSTRAINTS.description:
Displays whether constraint checking is done across stores:
TRUE/FALSE.

PARTITIONING_ON_NON_PRIMARY_KEY_COLUMNS.description:
Displays whether partitioning is allowed on a non-primary key column: TRUE/FALSE. This
value is only relevant for tables with time-selection
partitioning.

PRIMARY_KEY_UPDATE.description:
Displays whether UPDATE statements are allowed on primary key
columns: TRUE/FALSE.

DYNAMIC_RANGE_THRESHOLD.description:
Displays the threshold after which a new partition is created
dynamically.

EXTENDED_STORAGE_ENABLE_DELTA.description:
Displays whether extended storage is enabled to use a
delta store: TRUE, FALSE, or NULL. NULL means that the system
couldn't retrieve the status from extended storage.

CHANGE_ID.description: Displays the order of database installations.

CHANGE_TIME.description:
Displays the timestamp when the database was upgraded or recovered to the current
version.

SYSTEM_ID.description: Displays the system ID of the SAP HANA instance.

HOST.description:
Displays the host name of the catalog master. Only users with INIFILE ADMIN or CATALOG
READ privileges are able to view this column.

PINNED_SQL_PLANS.description: Provides information on currently pinned SQL plans and corresponding hints.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user who prepared the plan.

SESSION_USER_NAME.description:
Displays the session user name who prepared the plan. This is the same value as displayed in the
USER_NAME column in the M_CONNECTIONS monitored view.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name that the SQL plan belongs to.

IS_INTERNAL.description:
Displays whether the plan is executed from a database internal connection or a remote connection:
TRUE/FALSE.

ABAP_VARCHAR_MODE.description: Displays whether the value ABAP VARCHAR mode is enabled: TRUE/FALSE.

PIN_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp when the plan was pinned.

LAST_MODIFY_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp when the plan was modified.

HINT_STRING.description: Displays the text string to indicate a hint was applied.

SESSION_PROPERTIES.description: Displays the composed variables from the session context.

TYPE.description:
Displays the privilege type: SYSTEMPRIVILEGE, OBJECTPRIVILEGE,
PACKAGEPRIVILEGE, or APPLICATIONPRIVILEGE

PROCEDURES.description: Provides information about available stored procedures.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the stored procedure.

SQL_SECURITY.description:
Displays the SQL security setting of the stored procedure:
DEFINER/INVOKER

DEFAULT_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the unqualified objects in the procedure.

INPUT_PARAMETER_COUNT.description: Displays the input type parameter count.

OUTPUT_PARAMETER_COUNT.description: Displays the output type parameter count.

INOUT_PARAMETER_COUNT.description: Displays the in/out type parameter count.

IS_UNICODE.description: Displays whether the stored procedure contains Unicode: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_ENCRYPTED.description: Displays whether the stored procedure is encrypted: TRUE/FALSE.

DEFINITION.description: Displays the query string of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the stored procedure.

READ_ONLY.description: Displays whether the procedure is read-only: TRUE/FALSE

IS_VALID.description:
Displays whether the procedure is valid: TRUE/FALSE. A procedure become
invalid when its base objects are changed or dropped.

IS_HEADER_ONLY.description:
Displays whether the procedure is a header only procedure:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_DETERMINISTIC.description: Displays whether the stored procedure is deterministic: TRUE/FALSE.

HAS_TRANSACTION_CONTROL_STATEMENTS.description:
Displays whether the procedure has transaction control statements:
TRUE/FALSE.

AUTO_COMMIT_DDL.description:
Displays whether the stored procedure runs with autocommit DDL enabled:
TRUE/FALSE.

OWNER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the owner of the stored procedure.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time of the procedure.

PROCEDURE_OBJECTS.description: Contains the results of the system procedure GET_PROCEDURE_OBJECTS.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_NAME.description: Displays the procedure name of the stored procedure.

OBJECT_SCHEMA.description: Displays the schema name of the object in the stored procedure code.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name of the object in the stored procedure code.

OBJECT_TYPE_ID.description: Displays the type ID of the object in the stored procedure code.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the object in the stored procedure code.

START_POSITION.description:
Displays the start position of the object in the stored procedure
code.

END_POSITION.description: Displays the end position of the object in the stored procedure code.

PROCEDURE_PARAMETERS.description: Provides information about the stored procedure parameters.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the stored procedure.

SCALE.description: Displays the scale of the parameter.

POSITION.description: Displays the ordinal position of the parameter.

TABLE_TYPE_SCHEMA.description:
Displays the schema name of the table type if DATA_TYPE_NAME is
TABLE_TYPE.

TABLE_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the table type if DATA_TYPE_NAME is TABLE_TYPE.

IS_INPLACE_TYPE.description: Displays whether the parameter type is an inplace type: TRUE/FALSE.

PARAMETER_TYPE.description: Displays the parameter mode: IN, OUT, or INOUT.

HAS_DEFAULT_VALUE.description: Displays whether the parameter has a default value: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_NULLABLE.description: Displays whether the parameter accepts a NULL value: TRUE/FALSE.

PROCEDURE_PARAMETER_COLUMNS.description: Lists available columns of table parameters of stored procedures.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_NAME.description: Displays the same of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the stored procedure.

PARAMETER_POSITION.description: Displays the ordinal position of the parameter.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the column in the table parameter.

POSITION.description: Displays the ordinal position of the column in the table parameter.

DATA_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the SQL data type name of the column.

LENGTH.description:
Displays the number of characters for character types, the number of max digits for
numeric types, the number of charactesr for datetime types, or
the number of bytes for LOB types,

SCALE.description:
For numeric types, displays the maximum number of digits to the right of the decimal
point. For time and timestamp types, displays the decimal digits
defined as the number of digits to the right of the decimal
point in the second's component of the data.

IS_NULLABLE.description: Displays whether the column is allowed to accept NULL values: TRUE/FALSE.

PROJECTION_VIEW_COLUMN_SOURCES.description: Provides information about available projection view columns.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

SOURCE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the source table schema name.

PSES.description: Provides information about personal security environments (PSE).

PSE_ID.description: Displays the unique ID of the PSE.

NAME.description: Displays the name of the PSE.

PURPOSE.description: Displays the purpose of the PSE.

OWNER_NAME.description: Displays the owner of the PSE.

PSE_CERTIFICATES.description: Provides information about certificates used in PSEs.

PSE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the PSE.

CERTIFICATE_ID.description: Displays the unique ID of the certificate.

SUBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the subject of the X.509 certificate.

ISSUER_NAME.description: Displays the issuer of the X.509 certificate.

VALID_FROM.description: Displays the start time of certificate's validity.

VALID_UNTIL.description: Displays the end time of certificate's validity.

CERTIFICATE_USAGE.description: Displays the certificate usage: OWN, CHAIN, or TRUST.

CERTIFICATE_NAME.description: Displays the unique name of the certificate.

PSE_PUBLIC_KEYS.description: Provides information about all public keys in a PSE store.

PSE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the PSE.

PUBLIC_KEY_NAME.description: Displays the unique name of the public key.

PSE_PURPOSE_OBJECTS.description: Provides information about all PSEs and their assigned providers or hosts, referred to as purpose objects.

PSE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the PSE store.

PURPOSE.description: Displays the purpose of the PSE store.

PURPOSE_OBJECT.description:
For JWT or SAML purposes, displays the name of the assigned provider. For the SSL
purpose, this is the assigned host name.

PUBLIC_KEYS.description: Provides information about all public keys.

PUBLIC_KEY_NAME.description: Displays a unique name of the public key

ALGORITHM.description: Displays the algorithm of this key (RSA or EC)

ALGORITHM_DETAILS.description: Displays the keysize for RSA or the used curve for EC

KEY.description: Displays the PEM representation of the key (PKCS1 or PKCS8)

KEY_ID_HINT.description:
Displays the hint ID this key has in an external key management system (e.g.
JWKS)

COMMENT.description: Displays the description for the key

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation timestamp of the public key in the database

QUERY_PLANS.description: Plans how to handle query execution.

OPERATOR_DETAILS.description: Displays the detailed information on operators in the query plan.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name.

POSITION.description: Displays the position.

HOST.description: Displays the host where the plan operator is executed.

PORT.description: Displays the port where the plan operator is executed.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

POSITION.description: Displays the column position in this constraint.

REFERENCED_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the table referenced by this constraint.

REFERENCED_TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the table referenced by this constraint.

REFERENCED_COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the column referenced by this constraint.

REFERENCED_CONSTRAINT_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the unique constraint referenced by this
constraint.

UPDATE_RULE.description:
Displays the update rule: CASCADE, SET NULL, SET DEFAULT, or
RESTRICT.

DELETE_RULE.description:
Displays the delete rule: CASCADE, SET NULL, SET DEFAULT, or
RESTRICT.

IS_ENFORCED.description: Displays whether or not this constraint is enforced: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_VALIDATED.description: Displays whether or not this constraint is validated: TRUE/FALSE.

CHECK_TIME.description:
Displays the time when the constraint is checked:
INITIALLY_IMMEDIATE/INITIALLY DEFERRED.

IS_CDC_SUPPORTED.description: Displays whether or not the CDC is supported: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_REFRESH_OBJECTS_SUPPORTED.description: Displays whether or not refresh objects is supported: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_TREE.description:
Displays whether or not the browsing hierarchy is deeply nested:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_LINKED_DATABASE_SUPPORTED.description:
Displays whether or not the linked database is supported for this remote
source: TRUE/FALSE.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name.

OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the unique name to identify the remote source
object.

DISPLAY_NAME.description: Displays the name for the object.

IS_IMPORTABLE.description:
Displays whether or not the object is importable as a virtual
table: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_EXPANDABLE.description:
Displays whether or not the object can be expanded or browsed to
get inner objects: TRUE/FALSE.

PARENT_OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the parent object name for the specified object.

REMOTE_SOURCE_OBJECT_COLUMNS.description: Provides remote source object column information.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name.

OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the unique name to identify the remote source
object.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the column.

DATA_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the SAP HANA data type.

LENGTH.description:
Displays the length of the column. If there is a decimal then
this value is the precision.

IS_NULLABLE.description:
Displays whether or not this column is allowed to be NULL:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_AUTOINCREMENT.description:
Displays whether or not this column is automatically incremented:
TRUE/FALSE.

REMOTE_SOURCE_OBJECT_COLUMN_CONSTRAINTS.description: Provides remote source object column constraint information.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name.

OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the unique name to identify the remote source
object.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the column.

POSITION.description: Displays the position of the column.

IS_PRIMARY_KEY.description:
Displays whether or not the specified key is the primary key:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_UNIQUE_KEY.description:
Displays whether or not the specified key is unique:
TRUE/FALSE.

REMOTE_SOURCE_OBJECT_COLUMN_DESCRIPTIONS.description: Provides remote source object column description information.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name.

OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the unique name to identify the remote source
object.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the column.

DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the description of the specified column.

REMOTE_SOURCE_OBJECT_DESCRIPTIONS.description: Provides remote source object description information.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name.

OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the unique name to identify the remote source
object.

DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the description of the specified object.

REMOTE_SUBSCRIPTIONS.description: Lists all the remote subscriptions created for a remote source.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the remote subscription schema name.

IS_VALID.description:
Displays whether the remote subscription is valid or not:
TRUE/FALSE. This value is FALSE when its source or target
objects are changed or dropped.

VIRTUAL_TABLE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the virtual table schema name.

SUBSCRIPTION_QUERY_STRING.description:
Displays the SELECT statement specified in the subscription when
subscription type is SQL.

TARGET_OBJECT_TYPE.description:
Displays the remote subscription target object type: TABLE,
PROCEDURE, or TASK.

TARGET_OBJECT_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the target object schema name.

TARGET_OTHER_PARAM_STRING.description:
Displays the constant parameter string to pass during execution
when the target object type is PROCEDURE or TASK.

CHANGE_MODE.description:
Displays the remote subscription change mode: NULL, NORMAL,
UPSERT, or INSERT.

CHANGE_TYPE_COLUMN_NAME.description:
Displays the remote subscription change type column name in the
target table: I (INSERT), D (DELETE), and so on.

CHANGE_TIME_COLUMN_NAME.description:
Displays the remote subscription change time column name in the
target table. Supported data types are TIMESTAMP, CHAR, and
VARCHAR with a minimum length of 27.

CHANGE_SEQUENCE_COLUMN_NAME.description:
Displays the remote subscription change sequence column name in
the target table.

SCHEMA_CHANGES.description:
Displays whether or not the remote subscription propagates schema
changes: TRUE/FALSE.

TASK_PROCEDURE_PARAMETERS.description: Displays the comma-separated list of task parameters.

REMOTE_SUBSCRIPTION_DATA_CONTAINERS.description: Provides information regarding remote subscription data.

CONTENT_TYPE.description:
Displays the category of data containers:COMMIT SEQUENCE GROUPCOMMIT SEQUENCES (displays the order that transactions get committed and a sequence
number for each commit row)TRANSACTION (each container contains only one
transaction)LOBSROLLBACK SAVEPOINTSDISTRIBUTOR QUEUE DATA (displays the data currently being queued by the
distributor)DISTRIBUTOR QUEUE DATA REFERENCES (displays the list of transactions currently being
referenced by the distributor)

SUBSCRIPTION_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the remote subscription.

SUBSCRIPTION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the remote subscription.

TRANSACTION_ID.description: Displays the ID of the transaction being replicated.

CONTAINER_ID.description:
Displays the ID of the data container. Depending on the content type, a container can be
searched by REMOTE_SOURCE_ID and CONTENT_TYPE or with additional
information such as SUBSCRIPTION_OID or TRANSACTION_ID.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the object type: REMOTE_SOURCE/REMOTE_SUBSCRIPTION.

OBJECT_SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the schema name of the remote source or the remote
subscription, based on the OBJECT_TYPE.

OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the object name of the remote source or remote
subscription, based on the OBJECT_TYPE.

EXCEPTION_TIME.description: Displays the time at whoch the exception was raised.

COMPONENT.description: Displays the component that raised the exception.

REMOTE_USERS.description: Provides information about user mappings for cross-database access.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user in the local database.

REMOTE_USER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user in the remote database.

REMOTE_DATABASE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the remote database.

REORG_GENERATE_OVERVIEW.description: Tracks automated and administrator calls to the REORG_GENERATE procedure.

GENERATE_ID.description:
Displays the generated ID. This is an increasing, unique number used to identify the call
to the REORG_GENERATE procedure.

START_DATE.description: Displays the UTC timestamp when the call reached the engine.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID that initiates the call to the REORG_GENERATE procedure.

ALGORITHM_ID.description: Displays the table redistribution algorithm ID used the in the call.

REORG_ID.description:
Displays the reorganization ID used to select the corresponding executed table
redistribution plan.

PARAMETERS.description: Displays the table redistribution parameters that are used in the call.

DETAIL.description: Displays the detailed text for additional information.

REORG_OVERVIEW.description: Provides an overview of landscape redistributions.

REORG_ID.description: Displays the unique identifier for the executed reorg plan.

STATUS.description: Displays the status of the plan execution:STARTED: The plan execution has started.FINISHED: The plan execution finished successfully.FAILED: The plan execution is treated as failed.

START_DATE.description: Displays the UTC timestamp when the plan execution started.

END_DATE.description:
Displays the UTC timestamp when plan execution stopped. This field
remains empty while the execution is running or in the case of reboots
during plan execution.

USER.description: Displays the user that executed the table redistribution.

ALGORITHM_ID.description: Displays the ID of the algorithm for the table redistribution.

PARAMETERS.description: Displays the parameters given for the table redistribution call.

REORG_PLAN.description: Provides current plan information for landscape reorganization.

PRECONDITION.description: Displays the precondition of the specified step.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the table name.

NEW_HOST.description: Displays the host where the table/partition is moved to.

NEW_PORT.description: Displays the port where the table/partition is moved to.

OLD_HOST.description: Displays the host where the table/partition is located.

OLD_PORT.description: Displays the port where the table/partition is located.

REORG_PLAN_INFOS.description: Provides additional information about the current landscape reorganization plan.

REORG_STEPS.description: Contains the executed or to be executed table redistribution plan items.

STEP_GROUP.description:
Displays the ID of the table redistribution group of steps that the item
belongs to.

PRECONDITION.description:
Indicates the preconditions that have to be fulfilled before the table
redistribution step can be executed.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

NEW_HOST.description: Displays the host where the table/partition is moved to.

NEW_PORT.description: Displays the port where the table/partition is moved to.

OLD_HOST.description: Displays the host where the table/partition was located.

OLD_PORT.description: Displays the port where the table/partition was located.

STATUS.description: Displays the status message.

START_DATE.description: Specifies the UTC timestamp when the plan execution started.

END_DATE.description: Specifies the UTC timestamp when the plan execution stopped.

RESERVED_KEYWORDS.description: Provides a list of reserved keywords that are not allowed to be used as a simple identifier.

RESULT_CACHE.description: Provides information about objects available to use the result cache.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the object type: VIEW/FUNCTION.

CACHE_TYPE.description:
Displays which type of cache is enabled for the object: FULL, PARTIAL (SELECTED COLUMNS),
PARTIAL (FILTERED), or PARTIAL (SELECTED COLUMNS, FILTERED).

CACHE_RETENTION.description: Displays the cache refresh interval in minutes.

CACHE_FILTER.description: Displays the filter condition specified to reduce the cache size.

IS_CACHE_FORCED.description: Indicates whether the cached is forced or if cachability is checked: TRUE/FALSE.

RESULT_CACHE_COLUMNS.description: Provides information about columns available to use the result cache.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the object type.

CACHE_AGGREGATION_TYPE.description:
Indicates the required aggregation type in a query to use cache or indicate an
aggregation type for caching the column: SUM, MIN, MAX, or
COUNT.

ROLE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the name of the schema of the role granted.

ROLE_MODE.description: Displays the local mode of the role.

GLOBAL_IDENTITY.description: Displays the identity specified for the role with ROLE_MODE GLOBAL.

CREATOR.description: Displays the name of the user who created the role.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the time that the role was created.

COMMENTS.description: Displays the description for the specified role.

CONTEXT.description: Displays the context that the role is valid for.

ROLE_LDAP_GROUPS.description: Shows the mapping between SAP HANA roles and LDAP groups.

ROLE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the role.

ROLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the role.

LDAP_GROUP_NAME.description: Displays the name of the LDAP group.

SUBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the subject of the X.509 certificate of the SAML provider.

ISSUER_NAME.description: Displays the issuer of the X.509 certificate of the SAML provider.

ENTITY_ID.description: Displays the entity name of the SAML provider (optional).

IS_USER_CREATION_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether the provider is allowed to create a user implicitly:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_CASE_SENSITIVE.description:
Displays whether the user mapping is checked case sensitive:
TRUE/FALSE.

USER_CREATION_USER_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of user to create when user creation is enabled:
STANDARD, RESTRICTED

USER_CREATION_USERGROUP.description: Displays the user group a created user will be a member of.

USER_CREATION_AUTHORIZATION_MODE.description:
Displays the authorization mode of the automatic user creation. Possible
values are:NULL (automatic user creation is off)LDAPLOCAL

SAML_USER_MAPPINGS.description: Shows the SAML providers known for each user.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the user name.

EXTERNAL_IDENTITY.description: Displays the name of the user known to the provider.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the job.

SCHEDULER_JOB_NAME.description: Displays the name of the job.

PARAMETER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the parameter.

PARAMETER_VALUE.description: Displays the parameter value as a Unicode string.

USER_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the user who owns the job. The job is
executed as this user.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the job.

SCHEDULER_JOB_NAME.description: Displays the name of the job.

PROCEDURE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the called procedure.

PROCEDURE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the called procedure.

CRON.description: Displays the schedule of the job.

START_TIME.description:
If specified, displays the earliest time after which the
scheduled job can start to run; otherwise null.

END_TIME.description:
If specified, displays the latest time before which the scheduled
job can start to run; otherwise null.

IS_ENABLED.description: Displays whether the scheduled job can run: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_VALID.description:
Displays whether the job is valid: TRUE/FALSE. The job may become
invalid if the procedure becomes invalid or if the owner of the job
no longer has the EXECUTE privilege for the procedure. Invalid job
are not scheduled.

COMMENTS.description: Displays any comment on the job.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the time at which the job was created.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

HAS_PRIVILEGES.description:
Shows if the user is the schema owner or has any privileges for the
schema or any object within the schema: TRUE/FALSE.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time of the schema.

SEARCH_RULE_SETS.description: Shows information about available search rule sets.

SEARCH_RULE_SET_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the name of the runtime schema of the search rule set.

SEARCH_RULE_SET_PACKAGE_ID.description: Displays the name of the repository package of the search rule set.

SEARCH_RULE_SET_NAME.description: Displays the name of the search rule set.

SEARCHED_SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the schema name of the catalog object that is searched by the
given search rule set.

SEARCHED_OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the catalog object that is searched by the given
search rule set.

SEARCHED_REPOSITORY_PACKAGE_ID.description:
Displays the ID of the repository package of the repository object that
is searched by the given search rule set.

SEARCHED_REPOSITORY_OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the repository object that is searched by the given
search rule set.

SEARCH_RULE_SET_CONDITIONS.description: Shows conditions to available search rule sets.

SEARCH_RULE_SET_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the name of the runtime schema of the search rule set.

SEARCH_RULE_SET_PACKAGE_ID.description: Displays the name of the repository package of the search rule set.

SEARCH_RULE_SET_NAME.description: Displays the name of the search rule set.

RULE_NUMBER.description: Displays the number of the rule that contains the column.

RULE_ID.description: Displays the unique ID/name of the rule that contains the column.

SEARCHED_COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of a column that is used by a search rule set.

SEARCHED_COLUMN_CONDITION.description: Displays the condition: EQUALS, NOT EMPTY, or MISSING.

SEARCHED_COLUMN_VALUE.description:
Displays the value the user input is compared to, if condition is
EQUALS.

SEARCHED_COLUMN_VALUE_REPLACED_BY.description:
Displays the user input is replaced by this value if the action is
REPLACE.

ACTION.description:
Displays the action that is performed when the condition is true: SKIP
COLUMN, SKIP RULE, or REPLACE.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the sequence.

SEQUENCE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the sequence.

SEQUENCE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the sequence.

MIN_VALUE.description: Displays the minimum value of the sequence in bytes.

MAX_VALUE.description: Displays the maximum value of the sequence in bytes.

IS_CYCLED.description:
Displays whether the sequence starts with the MIN_VALUE after having
reached the MAX_VALUE in cases where INCREMENT_BY is greater than 0, or
if the sequence starts with the MAX_VALUE after having reached the
MIN_VALUE in cases where INCREMENT_BY is less than 0: TRUE/FALSE.

RESET_BY_QUERY.description: Displays the reset by query string for the sequence.

CACHE_SIZE.description: Displays the cache size of the sequence in bytes.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time of the sequence.

SERIES_KEY_COLUMNS.description: Provides information about series key columns for series tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the series key column name.

POSITION.description: Displays the ordinal position of the series key column.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

PERIOD_TYPE_ID.description: Displays the SQL data type ID of the period columns.

PERIOD_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the SQL data type name of the period columns.

IS_EQUIDISTANT.description: Displays the series table has equidistant elements: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_INSTANT.description:
Displays the series represents instants rather than a period with a start/end:
TRUE/FALSE.

INCREMENT_BY.description:
Displays the delta value for an equidistant series or NULL for a non-equidistant series.
When the PERIOD_TYPE is a numeric type, a string represents the
number. For datetime, a string in an interval form of<number-units>, where units means either
year, month, day, hour, minute, or second, represents the
number.

IS_MISSING_ALLOWED.description:
Indicates whether missing values are allowed in a non-equidistant series: TRUE/FALSE, or
NULL for a non-equidistant series.

PERIOD_START_COLUMN.description:
Displays the starting series column name, or NULL if it is an implied or an instant
series.

PERIOD_END_COLUMN.description:
Displays the ending series column name, or NULL if it is implied or an instant
series.

PERIOD_INSTANT_COLUMN.description: Displays the instant series column name, or NULL for an interval series.

MIN_VALUE.description: Displays the minimum value allowed in the series columns.

MAX_VALUE.description: Displays the maximum value allowed in the series columns.

SESSION_COOKIES.description: Shows information about available session cookies.

USER_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the user that the session cookie was created
for.

CLIENT_HOST.description: Displays the host name of client machine.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the time that the session cookie was created.

LAST_CONNECT_TIME.description:
Displays the last time that the session cookie was used for successful
reconnection.

SESSION_END_TIME.description: Displays the time that the main connection ended.

SQLSCRIPT_ANALYZER_RULES.description: Provides information regarding SQLScript Code Analyzer rules.

RULE_NAMESPACE.description: Displays the namespace for the rule.

CATEGORY.description:
Displays the classification of the rule: security, consistency,
performance, and so on.

SHORT_DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the short description of the rule.

LONG_DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the long description containing the rule background.

RECOMMENDATION.description: Displays the recommendation on how to handle the rule.

SQLSCRIPT_VARIABLE_CACHE.description: Provides information about procedures and functions that have variable caches defined.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the target object.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the target object name.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the target object type: PROCEDURE/FUNCTION.

VARIABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the variable to be cached.

ENABLE_MODE.description: Displays the activation mode: ENABLED, DISABLED, or AUTOMATIC.

STATEMENT_HINTS.description: Provides information about statement hints, including when they were last enabled and/or disabled and by whom.

STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the MD5 hash value for the STATEMENT_STRING column.

SYSTEM_HINT_STRING.description:
Displays the system hint string that conflicts with the existing user-defined hint string
(if a statement has both a user-defined hint and a system hint
associated with it, then only the user-defined hint is
effective).

COMMENTS.description:
Specifies comments configured for a statement hint (e.g., ALTER
SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINT.. COMMENT<comment>..)

IS_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether the hint is enabled. Possible values:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_OVERRIDE.description:
Specifies TRUE if an override is specified for the target query
(e.g., ALTER SYSTEM ADD STATEMENT HINT.. OVERRIDE...).

LAST_ENABLE_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp for when the hint was last enabled.

LAST_ENABLE_USER.description: Displays the user who enabled the hint.

LAST_DISABLE_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp for when the hint was last disabled.

LAST_DISABLE_USER.description: Displays the user who disabled the hint.

OBJECT_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of the target object the hint is being applied
to.

OBJECT_SCHEMA.description:
Displays the schema name of the target object the hint is being
applied to.

OBJECT_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the target object the hint is being applied
to.

STRUCTURED_PRIVILEGES.description: Provides information about available structured (analytic) privileges.

STRUCTURED_PRIVILEGE_ID.description: Displays the object ID of the structured privilege.

STRUCTURED_PRIVILEGE_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the privilege.

STRUCTURED_PRIVILEGE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the structured privilege.

RESTRICTION_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of restriction: CUBERESTRICTION, ACTIVITYRESTRICTION,
VALIDITYRESTRICTION, or DIMENSIONRESTRICTION.

DIMENSION_ATTRIBUTE.description: Displays the name of the dimension attribute.

FILTER_ID.description:
Displays the number of filters needed to combine all operators/operands
belonging to one filter.

FILTER_TYPE.description: Displays the type of filter: STATIC/DYNAMIC.

NEGATED.description: Indicates whether the operator is negated in the filter: TRUE/FALSE.

OPERATOR.description:
Displays the type of operator: CONTAINS PATTERN, BETWEEN, EQUAL, IN, LESS
THAN, LESS EQUAL, GREATER THAN, or GREATER EQUAL.

OPERAND_ORDER.description: Displays the sequence of the operands per filter ID.

OPERAND.description: Displays the value that the operator is compared to.

SCHEMA_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the schema to which the table containing the spatial
column belongs.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the table containing the spatial column.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the spatial column.

SRS_ID.description:
Displays the numeric identifier (SRID) for the spatial reference system
(SRS) associated with the spatial column.

SRS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the SRS associated with the spatial column.

COORD_DIMENSION.description: Displays the dimension of the spatial column.

INTERNAL_LAYOUT.description:
Displays the internal geometry layout: PLAIN or HILBERT CURVE. For
details see the column definition and column configuration.

SPATIAL_INDEX_PREFERENCE.description:
Displays the spatial index preference: DEFAULT, RDICT, or RTREE. For
details see the column definition and the column configuration.

VALIDATION.description:
Displays the geometry validation level: NONE (checked for bad
representation)/FULL (checked for all known invalid representations as
defined by the OGC standard).

HAS_BOUNDARY_CHECK.description:
Checks whether the geometries in the column are within the SRS
boundaries

ST_SPATIAL_REFERENCE_SYSTEMS.description: Provides information about spatial reference systems.

OWNER_NAME.description: Displays the owner name of the spatial reference system (SRS).

SRS_ID.description:
Displays the numeric identifier (SRID) of this spatial reference
system.

SRS_NAME.description: Displays the name of the spatial reference system.

ROUND_EARTH.description:
Indicates whether the SRS type is ROUND EARTH (TRUE) or PLANAR
(FALSE).

AXIS_ORDER.description:
Displays how the database server interprets points with regards to
latitude and longitude: x/y/z/m, long/lat/z/m, lat/long/z/m.

SNAP_TO_GRID.description:
Defines the granularity of the grid, in the LINEAR_UNIT_OF_MEASURE, that
geometries are snapped to on creation.

TOLERANCE.description: Defines the precision to use when comparing points.

SEMI_MAJOR_AXIS.description:
Displays the distance from the center of the ellipsoid to the equator for
a ROUND EARTH SRS.

SEMI_MINOR_AXIS.description:
Displays the distance from the center of the ellipsoid to the poles for a
ROUND EARTH SRS.

INV_FLATTENING.description:
Displays the inverse flattening used for the ellipsoid in a ROUND EARTH
SRS.

MIN_X.description: Displays the minimum x value allowed in coordinates.

MAX_X.description: Displays the maximum x value allowed in coordinates.

MIN_Y.description: Displays the minimum y value allowed in coordinates.

MAX_Y.description: Displays the maximum y value allowed in coordinates.

MIN_Z.description: Displays the minimum z value allowed in coordinates.

MAX_Z.description: Displays the maximum z value allowed in coordinates.

ORGANIZATION.description:
Displays the name of the organization that created the coordinate system
used by this spatial reference system.

ORGANIZATION_COORDSYS_ID.description:
Displays the ID given to the coordinate system by the organization that
created it.

SRS_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of SRS as defined by the SQL/MM standard: GEOGRAPHIC,
PROJECTED, or ENGINEERING.

LINEAR_UNIT_OF_MEASURE.description:
Displays the linear unit of measure used by this spatial reference
system.

ANGULAR_UNIT_OF_MEASURE.description:
Displays the angular unit of measure used by this spatial reference
system.

POLYGON_FORMAT.description:
Displays the orientation of the rings in a polygon: COUNTERCLOCKWISE,
CLOCKWISE, or EVENODD.

STORAGE_FORMAT.description:
Indicates whether the data is stored in normalized format (INTERNAL),
unnormalized format (ORIGINAL), or both (MIXED).

DEFINITION.description:
Displays the WKT definition of the spatial reference system in the format
defined by the OGC standard.

TRANSFORM_DEFINITION.description:
Displays the transform definition settings for use when transforming data
from this SRS to another.

ST_UNITS_OF_MEASURE.description: Provides information about spatial units of measure.

OWNER_NAME.description: Displays the owner name of this unit of measure.

UNIT_NAME.description: Displays the name of this unit of measure.

UNIT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of this unit of measure: ANGULAR/LINEAR.

CONVERSION_FACTOR.description: Displays the conversion factor of this unit of measure.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the synonym.

SYNONYM_NAME.description: Displays the name of the synonym.

SYNONYM_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the synonym.

OBJECT_DATABASE.description: Displays the database name of the referenced object.

OBJECT_SCHEMA.description: Displays the schema name of the referenced object.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the name of the referenced object.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the referenced object.

IS_COLUMN_OBJECT.description: Displays whether this view is a column object or not: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_VALID.description:
Displays whether the synonym is valid or not: TRUE/FALSE. This value
becomes FALSE when its base object is dropped.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time of the synonym.

TABLES.description: Provides information about tables in the database.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

FIXED_PART_SIZE.description: Displays the fixed part size of the table.

IS_LOGGED.description:
Displays whether or not logging was on for the table at last restart
time: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_COLUMN_TABLE.description: Displays whether the table is a column table: TRUE/FALSE.

TABLE_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the table: ROW, COLUMN, VIRTUAL,EXTENDED,or COLLECTION.

IS_INSERT_ONLY.description: Displays whether the table is an insert-only table: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_TENANT_SHARED_DATA.description:
Displays whether the table can be shared among other instances:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_TENANT_SHARED_METADATA.description: Displays whether the table is a global table: TRUE/FALSE.

SESSION_TYPE.description:
Displays the session type: NONE, SIMPLE, or HISTORY. For HISTORY, time
travel is possible.

IS_TEMPORARY.description: Displays whether the table is a temporary table: TRUE/FALSE.

COMMIT_ACTION.description: Displays the setting for on commit deletion for global temporary tables.

IS_USER_DEFINED_TYPE.description: Displays whether or not this is a user-defined table type: TRUE/FALSE.

HAS_PRIMARY_KEY.description:
Displays whether or not the table has a primary key: TRUE/FALSE. Only
valid for column store tables.

PARTITION_SPEC.description:
Displays the detail specification of table partitioning. Only valid for
column store tables.

USES_EXTKEY.description:
Displays whether or not the table uses an external key: TRUE/FALSE. Only
valid for column store tables.

AUTO_MERGE_ON.description:
Displays whether or not automatic delta table merge is activated for the
table: TRUE/FALSE. Only valid for column store tables.

USES_DIMFN_CACHE.description:
Displays whether or not the table uses the DimFunctionCache feature:
TRUE/FALSE. Only valid for column store tables.

IS_PUBLIC.description:
Displays whether or not the table is public: TRUE/FALSE. Only valid for
column store tables.

AUTO_OPTIMIZE_COMPRESSION_ON.description:
Displays whether or not automatic optimize compression is activated for
the table: TRUE/FALSE. Only valid for column store tables.

COMPRESSED_EXTKEY.description:
Displays whether or not the table uses a compressed external key:
TRUE/FALSE. Only valid for column store tables.

HAS_TEXT_FIELDS.description:
Displays whether or not the table has at least one column of type TEXT:
TRUE/FALSE. Only valid for column store tables.

USES_QUEUE_TABLE.description:
Displays whether or not the table uses a queue table: TRUE/FALSE. Only
valid for column store tables.

IS_PRELOAD.description:
Displays whether or not the table uses preloading: TRUE/FALSE. Only valid
for column store tables. Preload information can be NULL for row store, virtual columns, or tables on
extended storage.

IS_PARTIAL_PRELOAD.description:
Displays whether or not the table uses partial preloading: TRUE/FALSE.
Only valid for column store tables. Preload information can be NULL for
row store, virtual columns, or tables on
extended storage

UNLOAD_PRIORITY.description:
Displays the unload priority of the table: 0 - 9 means not unloadable, 1
means latest unload, and 9 means earliest unload.

HAS_SCHEMA_FLEXIBILITY.description:
Displays whether or not the table has schema flexibility: TRUE/FALSE.
Only valid for column store tables.

IS_REPLICA.description: Displays whether or not the table is a replica: TRUE/FALSE.

HAS_STRUCTURED_PRIVILEGE_CHECK.description:
Displays whether or not the table is registered for a structured
privilege check: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_SERIES_TABLE.description: Displays whether or not the table is a series table: TRUE/FALSE.

ROW_ORDER_TYPE.description: Displays the row order type: NULL/BY VALUE.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time of the table.

TEMPORAL_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of temporal table:TEMPORAL - a table with system versioning enabled. Tables of this
type have a corresponding entry in the TEMPORAL_TABLES system
table.HISTORY - the history table associated with a system-versioned
table.NULL - all other types of tables.

HAS_MASKED_COLUMNS.description:
Displays whether or not the table has a mask definition for at least one
column: TRUE/FALSE.

MASK_MODE.description:
Displays the mask mode to be applied if the table has masked columns:
DEFAULT or SESSION USER; otherwise NULL.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays the user-specified persistent memory preference: TRUE
(persistent memory is ON)/FALSE (persistent memory OFF). If the user has
not specified a persistent memory preference, or if the preference is set
to the default, then the value is NULL.

HAS_RECORD_COMMIT_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays whether the table is tracking commit timestamps: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_REPLICATION_LOG_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether the table's replication log is collected. Values are
TRUE/FALSE.

NUMA_NODE_INDEXES.description:
Displays a comma-separated list of user-specified logical NUMA node
indexes for the table. For each column entry in this view there are
preferences (if set); otherwise, the value is NULL.The elements of the list are either individual nodes indexes or ranges of
index nodes, or a mixture of both. For example, the value0, 1 TO 3, 6indicates node indexes 0, 1,
2, 3, and 6.

IS_MOVABLE.description:
Displays whether the table can be moved to another location:
TRUE/FALSE.

LOAD_UNIT.description:
Displays the load unit for the table partition: TABLE, PAGE (when the
partition uses paged attributes), COLUMN, or DEFAULT.

TABLE_COLUMNS.description: Provides information about available table columns.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the column.

POSITION.description: Indicates the ordinal position of the column in a record.

DATA_TYPE_ID.description: Displays the SQL data type ID of the column.

DATA_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the SQL data type name of the column.

OFFSET.description: Displays the offset of the column in the record.

LENGTH.description:
Indicates the number of chars for CHAR types, number of max digits for
numeric types, number of chars for datetime types, and number of bytes
for LOB types.

SCALE.description:
Indicates the numeric types: the maximum number of digits to the right of
the decimal point; time, timestamp: the decimal digits are defined as the
number of digits to the right of the decimal point in the second's
component of the data.

IS_NULLABLE.description:
Displays whether the column is allowed to accept null values: TRUE or
FALSE.

DEFAULT_VALUE.description: Displays the default value of the column.

COLLATION.description: Displays the collation used for this column.

COMMENTS.description: Displays the description for this column.

CS_DATA_TYPE_ID.description: Displays the column store data type ID.

CS_DATA_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the column store data type name.

DDIC_DATA_TYPE_ID.description: Displays the DDIC data type ID.

DDIC_DATA_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the DDIC data type name.

COMPRESSION_TYPE.description:
Indicates the type of compression: DEFAULT, PREFIXED, SPARSE, CLUSTERED,
SPARSE, CLUSTERED, INDIRECT, RLE, and LINEAR RLE. The default value for
the column is shown. The actual value can be checked in the system view
M_ CS_COLUMNS. For row store columns, NONE is shown.

INDEX_TYPE.description: Indicates the type of index: NONE or FULL.

COLUMN_ID.description: Displays the ID of the column.

PRELOAD.description:
Displays if the column is preloaded: TRUE or FALSE. Preload information
can be NULL in case of row store, virtual
columns, or tables on extended storage.

GENERATED_ALWAYS_AS.description: Displays the expression of the column created by GENERATED... AS.

HAS_SCHEMA_FLEXIBILITY.description: Displays if the column has schema flexibility: TRUE or FALSE.

FUZZY_SEARCH_INDEX.description: Displays if the column has a fuzzy search index: TRUE or FALSE.

MEMORY_THRESHOLD.description: Displays the memory threshold in bytes for LOB types.

LOAD_UNIT.description:
Displays the unit of column data loading: TABLE, COLUMN, PAGE, or
DEFAULT.

GENERATION_TYPE.description:
Displays ALWAYS AS if the column is generation column, ALWAYS AS
IDENTITY, BY DEFAULT AS IDENTITY if the column is an identity column
whose values are always generated or generated by default, and NULL
otherwise.For system-versioning columns, the value 'ALWAYS AS ROW START' indicates
a column that was defined as GENERATED ALWAYS AS ROW START, while the
value 'ALWAYS AS ROW END' indicates a column that was defined as
GENERATED ALWAYS AS ROW END.

IS_CACHABLE.description: Displays whether the column is part of cached data: TRUE or FALSE.

IS_CACHE_KEY.description: Displays whether the column is part of cache key: TRUE or FALSE.

IS_HIDDEN.description: Displays whether the column is hidden: TRUE or FALSE.

IS_MASKED.description: Displays whether the column is masked: TRUE or FALSE.

MASK_EXPRESSION.description:
Displays the mask expression. It is only visible to users with the DATA
ADMIN and CATALOG READ privileges, the SELECT METADATA privilege on the
schema, and who are the owner of the table or schema.

CLIENTSIDE_ENCRYPTION_STATUS.description:
Displays whether the column is undergoing encryption (ENCRYPTING),
decryption (DECRYPTING), re-encryption with a different column encryption
key (REENCRYPTING), re-encryption with the latest version of the existing
column encryption key (ACTIVATING), is already encrypted (ENCRYPTED), or
is in clear-text (NULL).

CLIENTSIDE_ENCRYPTION_COLUMN_KEY_ID.description:
Displays the UUID of the column encryption key used to encrypt the
column. The value is NULL if the column is not encrypted.

CLIENTSIDE_ENCRYPTION_MODE.description:
Displays the encryption mode. Possible values are RANDOM, DETERMINISTIC,
or NULL (not encrypted).

PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays the user-specified persistent memory preference: TRUE
(persistent memory is ON), or FALSE (persistent memory OFF). If the user
has not specified a persistent memory preference, or if the preference is
set to the default, then the value is NULL.

NUMA_NODE_INDEXES.description:
Displays a comma-separated list of user-specified logical NUMA node
indexes for the table columns. For each column entry in this view there
will be preferences (if set); otherwise, the value is NULL.The elements of the list are either individual nodes indexes or ranges of
index nodes, or a mixture of both. For example, the value0, 1 TO 3, 6indicates node indexes 0, 1,
2, 3, and 6.

TABLE_COLUMNS_ODBC.description: Provides information about available table columns.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

TABLE_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the table.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the column.

DATA_TYPE_ID.description: Displays the SQL data type ID of the column.

DATA_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the SQL data type name of the column.

COLUMN_SIZE.description:
Displays the total number of characters required to display the value
when it is converted to characters.

BUFFER_LENGTH.description: Displays the length, in bytes, required to transfer the value.

DECIMAL_DIGITS.description:
Displays the total number of significant digits to the right of the
decimal point.

NUM_PREC_RADIX.description:
Displays how to interpret columns COLUMN_SIZE and DECIMAL_DIGITS. For
numeric data types either 10 or 2.

NULLABLE.description:
Displays whether the column is allowed to accept null value:
TRUE/FALSE.

COLUMN_DEF.description: Displays the default value of the column.

SQL_DATA_TYPE.description: Displays the SQL data type ID of the column.

SQL_DATETIME_SUB.description: Displays the subtype code for datetime data types.

CHAR_OCTET_LENGTH.description:
Displays the maximum length, in bytes, of a character or binary data type
column.

POSITION.description: Displays the ordinal position of the column in a record.

COMMENTS.description: Displays the description for the column.

TABLE_GROUPS.description: Provides an overview of table group relationships.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

IS_GROUP_LEAD.description: Determines the leading table within a group.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

NODE_ID.description: Displays the ID of the partition node.

PARENT_NODE_ID.description: Displays the ID of the parent node.

PART_ID.description:
Returns the table partition ID:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.For replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica
tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.The part ID is NULL if the row describes the partitioning of
a higher level node in multi-level heterogenous partitioning
that has child nodes.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is currently in progress.

LEVEL_1_PARTITION.description:
Displays the first level partition ID. Possible values are 1 through the number of
first-level partitions. First-level partitions (those used in ALTER
TABLE...MOVE) may have subpartitions. Displays the logical partition
ID. Possible values are 1 through the number of partitions for
partitioned tables. This is the ID shown in all monitoring views.

LEVEL_2_PARTITION.description:
Displays the subpartition ID. Possible values are 0 for tables without multilevel
partitioning and 1 through the number of subpartitions for
partitioned tables with multilevel partitioning.

LEVEL_1_RANGE_MIN_VALUE.description:
Displays the minimum value of the range partition at the first level for a
range-partitioned table; empty otherwise.

LEVEL_1_RANGE_MAX_VALUE.description:
Displays the exclusive maximum value of the range partition at the first level for a
range-partitioned table; empty otherwise. This value is not included
in the range.

LEVEL_2_RANGE_MIN_VALUE.description:
Displays the minimum value of the range partition at the second level for a
range-partitioned table; empty otherwise.

LEVEL_2_RANGE_MAX_VALUE.description:
Displays the exclusive maximum value of the range partition at the second level for a
range-partitioned table; empty otherwise. This value is not included
in the range.

IS_CURRENT.description: Displays whether the partition is the current partition: TRUE/FALSE.

UNIQUE_CONSTRAINTS.description:
Displays if unique constraints are checked for this partition or not:
TRUE/FALSE.

DYNAMIC_RANGE_THRESHOLD.description:
Displays the threshold after which a new partition will be
created dynamically.

LOAD_UNIT.description:
Displays the load unit for partition: PAGE (when the partition uses page loadable
storage), COLUMN (when the partition uses column loadable
storage), or DEFAULT.

INSERT.description: Displays whether INSERT statements are allowed on the partition: TRUE/FALSE.

STORAGE_TYPE.description: Displays the storage type for the partition: DEFAULT/EXTENDED.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays the user-specified persistent memory preference: TRUE (persistent memory is
ON)/FALSE (persistent memory OFF). If the user has not specified
a persistent memory preference, or if the preference is set to
the default, then the value is NULL.

NUMA_NODE_INDEXES.description:
Displays a comma-separated list of user-specified logical NUMA node indexes for the table
partition. The elements of the list are either individual nodes
indexes or ranges of index nodes, or a mixture of both. For
example, the value0, 1 TO 3, 6indicates node indexes 0, 1, 2, 3, and 6.

DYNAMIC_RANGE_INTERVAL.description:
Displays the interval value and type by which new partitions can
be created dynamically.

IS_DYNAMIC.description: Displays whether a partition is dynamic: (TRUE/FALSE).

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

MIN_ROWS_FOR_PARTITIONING.description: Displays the minimum number of rows for partitioning.

INITIAL_PARTITIONS.description: Displays the initial number of partitions.

SAME_PARTITION_COUNT.description:
Displays whether to use the same count of partitions for each table belonging to the
group: TRUE/FALSE.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY.description:
Displays whether or not the data is loaded in persistent memory:
TRUE/FALSE.

PAGE_LOADABLE.description: Displays whether or not the data is page loadable: TRUE/FALSE.

REPLICA_COUNT.description: Displays the value of the required number of replicas.

NUMA_NODE_INDEXES.description: Displays the value of the allowed numa nodes.

REPLICA_TYPE.description: Displays the value of the allowed replica type.

INCLUDE.description:
Displays the list of volume IDs or synonyms which are added to
the respective location name.

EXCLUDE.description:
Displays the list of volume IDs or synonyms which are removed
from the respective location name.

TABLE_REPLICAS.description: Provides information about replicated tables and their replicas.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

HAS_DIFFERENT_PARTITIONS.description:
Displays whether the replica table has different partitions from the
source table.

HAS_DIFFERENT_COLUMNS.description:
Displays whether the replica table has different columns from the source
table.

TASK_OID.description: Displays the unique identifier for the task.

TASK_NAME.description: Displays the name of the task.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema that the task was created in.

OWNER_NAME.description: Displays the owner of the task.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time.

MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory size of loaded task in bytes.

TASK_TYPE.description: Displays the type of task derived from the task plan.

PLAN_VERSION.description: Displays the version of the task plan.

PLAN.description:
Displays the task plan used to define the task. This value is
NULL if the task was created using a procedure.

COMMENTS.description: Displays the description of the task from the task plan.

HAS_TABLE_TYPE_INPUT.description:
Displays whether or not the task is modeled with a table type as
input: TRUE/FALSE. If TRUE, this means that data would need to
be pushed at execution time.

HAS_SDQ.description:
Displays whether or not the task contains SDQ (Smart Data
Quality) functionality: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_REALTIME_TASK.description:
Displays whether or not the specified task is a realtime
task.

IS_VALID.description:
Displays whether or not the task is in a valid state: TRUE/FALSE.
FALSE indicates that the task has been invalidated by a
dependency.

IS_READ_ONLY.description:
Displays whether or not the task is read only, meaning it has
only table type outputs: TRUE/FALSE. FALSE indicates that the
task writes to non-table-type outputs.

PROCEDURE_SCHEMA.description:
If the task was created with a procedure instead of a plan,
contains the schema name of the stored procedure.

PROCEDURE_NAME.description:
If the task was created with a procedure instead of a plan,
contains the name of the stored procedure.

INPUT_PARAMETER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of input (tableType) parameters.

OUTPUT_PARAMETER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of output (tableType) parameters.

SQL_SECURITY.description: Displays the security model for the task: DEFINER/INVOKER.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema that the task was created in.

TASK_NAME.description: Displays the name of the task.

PARAMETER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the parameter.

POSITION.description: Displays the position of the parameter.

TABLE_TYPE_SCHEMA.description: Displays the schema that the table type was created in.

TABLE_TYPE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the table type.

TEMPORAL_TABLES.description: Provides information about system-versioned tables, history tables, and application-time period tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

PERIOD_START_COLUMN.description: Displays the period start column name.

PERIOD_END_COLUMN.description: Displays the period end column name.

HISTORY_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the history table associated to the system-versioned table.

HISTORY_TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the history table associated to the system-versioned table.

TEXT_CONFIGURATIONS.description: Provides information about configurations and other resources for customizing text analysis and text mining.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the name of the schema containing the resource.

NAME.description: Displays the name of the resource.

TYPE.description:
Displays the resource type: hdbtextconfig (configuration), textdict (dictionary),
textrule (ruleset), textminingconfig, and so on.

OWNER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the resource owner.

LAST_UPDATE_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp when the resource was created or last updated.

TIMEZONES.description: Provides information about the timezones which are available together with their originating data set.

TIMEZONE_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the respective timezone that can be used as parameter to give the
source respective destination timezone for UTCTOLOCAL and
LOCALTOUTC.

TIMEZONE_DATASET.description:
Displays the data set in which the timezones definition is located. It can take the
values sap or platform as they are allowed for the dataset
parameter to UTCTOLOCAL/LOCALTOUTC. The value of this column can
thus be directly used as an input for the respective
UTCTOLOCAL/LOCALTOUTC parameter.

TRANSACTION_HISTORY.description: Provides information about committed transactions and their users.

COMMIT_ID.description: Displays the ID of the committed transaction.

APP_USER.description: Displays the user that used the transaction.

TRIGGERS.description: Provides information about triggers that are defined for tables.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the trigger.

TRIGGER_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the trigger.

OWNER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the trigger owner.

OWNER_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the trigger owner.

SUBJECT_TABLE_SCHEMA.description: Displays the schema name of the table, the trigger is defined for.

SUBJECT_TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the table name of the table, the trigger is defined for.

TRIGGER_ACTION_TIME.description:
Displays the time the trigger is executed: BEFORE, AFTER, or INSTEAD OF
the specified event.

TRIGGER_EVENT.description:
Displays the event the trigger is defined for: combination of DELETE,
INSERT, or UPDATE.

TRIGGERED_ACTION_LEVEL.description:
Displays the level of the event where the triggered action happens:
ROW/STATEMENT.

DEFINITION.description: Displays the query string of the trigger.

IS_VALID.description:
Displays whether the trigger is valid or not: TRUE/FALSE. This becomes
FALSE when its base objects are changed or dropped.

IS_ENABLED.description: Displays whether the trigger is enabled or not: TRUE/FALSE.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the time that the trigger was created.

TRIGGER_ORDERS.description: Provides information about trigger order for triggers in the database.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the trigger.

TRIGGER_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the trigger.

REFERENCED_SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name of the referenced trigger.

REFERENCED_TRIGGER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the referenced trigger.

REFERENCED_TRIGGER_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the referenced trigger.

ORDER_TYPE.description: Displays the type of ordering: FOLLOWS/PRECEDES.

USERGROUPS.description: Provides details on all user groups.

USERGROUP_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user group.

CREATOR.description: Displays the creator of the user group.

IS_USER_ADMIN_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether a user with the USER ADMIN system privilege can manage the specified
user group: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_CLIENT_CONNECT_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether a user in this user group is able to connect to
the database: TRUE/FALSE.

HAS_CONNECT_RESTRICTION.description:
Displays whether connect restrictions are defined for this user
group: TRUE/FALSE.

COMMENTS.description: Displays the description for the specified user group.

USERGROUP_CONNECT_RESTRICTIONS.description: Provides details on connection restrictions for all user groups.

USERGROUP_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user group.

RESTRICTION_NAME.description: Displays the name of the connect restriction.

IS_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether the connect restriction is enabled or not.
Possible values are: TRUE / FALSE.

RESTRICTION_TYPE.description:
Displays the type of the restriction. Possible values are: IP, APPLICATION, or
APPLICATION DENY.

VALUE.description: Displays a network address or application name, depending on the RESTRICTION_TYPE.

USERGROUP_PARAMETERS.description: Provides the list of parameter sets defined for usergroups.

USERGROUP_NAME.description: Displays the name of the usergroup.

PARAMETER_SET_NAME.description: Displays the name of the parameter set the parameter belongs to.

IS_PARAMETER_SET_ENABLED.description: Displays if the parameter set is enabled for the usergroup: TRUE/FALSE.

PARAMETER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the parameter.

PARAMETER_VALUE.description: Displays the value of the parameter.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user.

USER_ID.description: Displays the ID of the user.

USERGROUP_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the user group that the user belongs to; otherwise,
NULL.

USER_MODE.description: Displays the mode of the user: LOCAL/EXTERNAL.

EXTERNAL_IDENTITY.description: Displays the external identity of the user.

CREATOR.description:
Displays the creator of the user, SYSTEM, or the ID of a user with USER
ADMIN privilege. If the user was created by an LDAP provider, the value
is SYS.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the creation time.

VALID_FROM.description: Displays the start time of the user's validity.

VALID_UNTIL.description: Displays the end time of the user's validity.

LAST_SUCCESSFUL_CONNECT.description: Displays the time of the last successful connection of the user.

LAST_INVALID_CONNECT_ATTEMPT.description: Displays the time of the last invalid connection attempt.

INVALID_CONNECT_ATTEMPTS.description:
Displays the number of invalid connection attempts since the last
successful connection.

ADMIN_GIVEN_PASSWORD.description:
Displays whether the password was provided by the administrator or by the
user: TRUE/FALSE.

LAST_PASSWORD_CHANGE_TIME.description: Displays the time of the last password change.

PASSWORD_CHANGE_NEEDED.description:
Displays whether the user is forced to change their own password:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_PASSWORD_LIFETIME_CHECK_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether the password-lifetime will be checked for the user:
TRUE/FALSE.

USER_DEACTIVATED.description: Displays whether the user is deactivated: TRUE/FALSE.

DEACTIVATION_TIME.description:
Displays the time given with an explicit deactivation command for the
specified user.

IS_PASSWORD_ENABLED.description: Displays whether authentication using a password is enabled: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_KERBEROS_ENABLED.description: Displays whether authentication using KERBEROS is enabled: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_SAML_ENABLED.description: Displays whether authentication using SAML is enabled: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_X509_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether authentication using an X.509 certificate is enabled:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_SAP_LOGON_TICKET_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether authentication using an SAP logon ticket is enabled:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_SAP_ASSERTION_TICKET_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether authentication using an SAP assertion ticket is enabled:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_RESTRICTED.description:
Indicates whether the user is missing the PUBLIC role and privilege on
their own schema: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_RESTRICTED_DETAILS.description:
Displays the missing privilege(s): ROLE PUBLIC/CREATE ANY ON OWN
SCHEMA.

IS_CLIENT_CONNECT_ENABLED.description:
Displays whether the user is allowed to connect outside of applications:
TRUE/FALSE.

HAS_REMOTE_USERS.description: Displays whether the user has a remote identity mapping: TRUE/FALSE.

AUTHORIZATION_MODE.description: Displays the Authorization mode of the user: LOCAL/LDAP.

IS_JWT_ENABLED.description: Displays whether authentication using JWT is enabled: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_LDAP_ENABLED.description: Displays whether authentication using LDAP is enabled:TRUE/FALSE.

CREATE_PROVIDER_TYPE.description:
If the user was created by a provider using automatic user provisioning,
then value indicates the type of provider: LDAP PROVIDER/SAML PROVIDER.
If the user was created by another user with USER ADMIN privilege (using
a CREATE USER statement), then the value is NULL.

CREATE_PROVIDER_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the provider that created the user, or NULL if the
user was created by a user with USER ADMIN privilege (using a CREATE USER
statement).

USER_PARAMETERS.description: Lists all parameters and their values, which have been assigned to users in the system (using CREATE USER ... SET PARAMETER or ALTER USER ... SET PARAMETER).

USER_NAME.description: Displays the name of the user.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema name.

VIEW_OID.description: Displays the object ID of the view.

IS_UNICODE.description: Displays whether the query string contains Unicode: TRUE/FALSE.
