INSTANCE_TOTAL_MEMORY_PEAK_USED_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool used by SAP HANA processes
since the instance started (this is a sample-based value) in bytes.

INSTANCE_TOTAL_MEMORY_ALLOCATED_SIZE.description:
Displays the size of the memory pool for all SAP HANA processes in
bytes.

INSTANCE_CODE_SIZE.description:
Displays the code size, including shared libraries of SAP HANA processes
in bytes.

INSTANCE_SHARED_MEMORY_ALLOCATED_SIZE.description: Displays the shared memory size of SAP HANA processes in bytes.

TOTAL_CPU_USER_TIME.description: Displays the CPU time spent in the user mode in milliseconds.

TOTAL_CPU_SYSTEM_TIME.description: Displays the CPU time spent in the kernel mode in milliseconds.

TOTAL_CPU_WIO_TIME.description:
Displays the CPU time spent in wait I/O in milliseconds. Linux only,
Microsoft Windows is always 0.

TOTAL_CPU_IDLE_TIME.description: Displays the CPU idle time in milliseconds.

SYS_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the host timestamp in the local time zone.

UTC_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the host timestamp in UTC.

OPEN_FILE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of allocated file handles on the host. The Kernel
parameter isfs.file-nr. For more information, see
the OPEN_FILE_LIMIT key in the M_HOST_INFORMATION system view topic.

ACTIVE_ASYNC_IO_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of asynchronous input and/or output requests on the
host. The Kernel parameter isfs.ai-nr. For more
information, see the ASYNC_IO_LIMIT key in the M_HOST_INFORMATION system
view topic.

FAST_RESTART_MEMORY_MAPPED_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of memory from the memory pool dedicated to the fast
restart option (FRO) that is mapped to SAP HANA processes.

FAST_RESTART_MEMORY_FILE_SIZE.description:
Displays the size of the memory pool dedicated to the fast restart option
(FRO) that is owned by all SAP HANA processes.

FAST_RESTART_MEMORY_PEAK_MAPPED_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool dedicated to the fast
restart option (FRO) that was mapped to SAP HANA processes since the
start of the instance (sample-based value).

FAST_RESTART_MEMORY_PEAK_FILE_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool dedicated to the fast
restart option (FRO) that was owned by SAP HANA processes since the start
of the instance (sample-based value).

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_MAPPED_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of memory from the memory pool dedicated to
persistent memory that is currently mapped to SAP HANA processes.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_FILE_SIZE.description:
Displays the amount of the memory pool dedicated to persistent memory
that is owned by all SAP HANA processes.

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_PEAK_MAPPED_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool dedicated to persistent
memory that was mapped to SAP HANA processes since the start of the
instance (sample-based value).

PERSISTENT_MEMORY_PEAK_FILE_SIZE.description:
Displays the peak memory from the memory pool dedicated to persistent
memory that was owned by SAP HANA processes since the start of the instance
(sample-based value).

M_IMPORT_BINARY_STATUS.description: Provides import status information for the current session.

CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the connection ID to distinguish data of each import session (-1
for Python).

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the table or view being imported.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the table or view being imported.

INDEX_TYPE.description:
Displays the index type: UNKNOWN, PHYSICAL, OLAP, JOIN, HIERARCHY, or
CALCULATION.

STATUS.description:
Displays the import status: QUEUED, WORKING, SKIPPED, DONE, or
FAILED.

ERROR.description:
Displays empty if the import was successful and displays error text
otherwise.

M_INDEXING_QUEUES.description: Provides the status of indexing queues.

QUEUE_TYPE.description: Displays the type of the queue: TEXT/GEOCODE.

INDEX_OID.description: Displays the OID of the corresponding index.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the data table.

TABLE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the data table.

PART_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the
original table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to
replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is in progress.

COLUMN_NAME.description: Displays the name of the document column.

STATUS.description: Displays the queue status: ACTIVE/SUSPENDED.

TOTAL_DOCUMENT_COUNT.description: Displays the total number of documents in the original table.

INDEXED_DOCUMENT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of successfully indexed documents.

QUEUE_DOCUMENT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of documents in the queue.

ERROR_DOCUMENT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of failed documents.

M_INIFILES.description: Provides information about all configuration files.

DEFAULT_LAYER.description:
Indicates whether or not the file has configuration on the default layer.
This value is always TRUE.

SYSTEM_LAYER.description:
Indicates whether or not the file has configuration on the system layer:
TRUE/FALSE.

DATABASE_LAYER.description:
Indicates whether or not the file has configuration on the database
layer: TRUE/FALSE.

HOST_LAYER.description:
Indicates whether or not the file has configuration on the host layer:
TRUE/FALSE.

M_INIFILE_CONTENTS.description: Provides configuration information from INI files.

LAYER_NAME.description: Displays the configuration layer: DEFAULT, SYSTEM, HOST or DATABASE.

HOST.description:
Displays the host name, if the LAYER_NAME is HOST. Otherwise, this value
is NULL.

M_INIFILE_CONTENT_HISTORY.description: Provides change history information for configuration (ini) files.

TIME.description:
Displays the timestamp when the configuration setting was
changed.

FILE_NAME.description: Displays the name of the configuration file.

LAYER_NAME.description:
Displays the configuration layer: DEFAULT, SYSTEM, HOST, or
DATABASE.

HOST.description:
Displays the host name, if the LAYER_NAME is HOST. Otherwise,
this value is NULL.

USER_NAME.description:
Displays the user name if the change was made by an SAP HANA
user.

APPLICATION_NAME.description:
Displays the value of the APPLICATION session variable, if an
application made the change. Otherwise, this value is NULL.

APPLICATION_USER_NAME.description:
Displays the value of the APPLICATIONUSER session variable, if an
application made the change. Otherwise, this value is NULL.

APPLICATION_SOURCE.description:
Displays the name of the application source if an application
made the change. Otherwise, this value is NULL.

SECTION.description:
Displays the name of the section in the configuration file where
the change was made.

KEY.description: Displays the name of the configuration key that was changed.

PREV_VALUE.description:
Displays the value of the configuration parameter before the
change was made.

COMMENTS.description: Displays the comment provided, if any, at the time of the configuration change.

MAX_CONCURRENCY_CONFIG.description:
Displays the maximum limit configured for concurrently running worker
threads.If this limit is exceeded, a dynamic reduction in the concurrently
running worker threads is automatically triggered, to prevent overload.

MAX_CONCURRENCY.description:
Displays the maximum number of concurrently running worker threads.The job executor keeps MAX_CONCURRENCY worker threads busy. As a result,
more worker threads are started if some worker threads are in a wait
state for some time. Even if these additional worker threads are
terminated, some of them are kept for later use and counted in
PARKED_WORKER_COUNT.

TOTAL_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of worker threads, including one extra worker
thread for emergency diagnostic purposes.

PARKED_WORKER_COUNT.description: Displays the number of parked worker threads.

FREE_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the total number of worker threads that are idle and may
immediately take up a job to work on.

SYS_WAITING_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of worker threads waiting in system. A worker thread
may wait for any other kind of synchronization.

JOB_WAITING_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of worker threads waiting for a job on another worker
thread.

YIELD_WAITING_WORKER_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of worker threads waiting on a yield. Sometimes a job
running on a worker thread yields execution to another job that is more
important.

TOTAL_WAITING_JOB_COUNT.description: Displays the number of all jobs waiting for execution.

QUEUED_WAITING_JOB_COUNT.description: Displays the number of jobs queued for execution.

WORKER_CREATE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of threads created.

M_JOBEXECUTORS_RESET.description: Provides values accumulated since the last reset of the main view M_JOBEXECUTORS.

M_JOB_HISTORY_INFO.description: Provides a history of long running system operations.

DISPLAY_LINE_COLOR.description: Displays the recommended display line color as RGB.

DISPLAY_LINE_STYLE.description: Displays the recommended display line style: 1=solid, 2=dotted, 3=dashed.

M_JOB_PROGRESS.description: Provides information about current long running system operations.

SCHEMA_NAME.description: Displays the schema of the object.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the name of the object.

JOB_NAME.description: Displays the type of operation. See the table below for more detail.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection that triggered the operation.

START_TIME.description: Displays the start time of the operation.

CURRENT_PROGRESS.description: Displays the steps of the operation that are already finished.

PROGRESS_DETAIL.description: Displays the detailed information about the operation.

M_JOINENGINE_STATISTICS.description: Provides statistics about join engine runtime objects used for column store join operations.

TRANSLATION_TABLE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of translation tables.

TRANSLATION_TABLE_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory used by translation tables in bytes.

TRANSLATION_TABLE_UNLOAD_COUNT.description: Displays the number of translation table unloads.

M_JOIN_DATA_STATISTICS.description: Provides column store join engine join statistics.

SCHEMA_NAME1.description: Displays the schema name of the first table

TABLE_NAME1.description: Displays the name of the first table

COLUMN_NAME1.description: Displays the column name of the first table

SCHEMA_NAME2.description: Displays the schema name of the second table. Join data statistics are not collected

TABLE_NAME2.description: Displays the name of the second table.

COLUMN_NAME2.description: Displays the column name of the second table.

LAST_REFRESH_TIME.description: Displays the time when the statistics was last refreshed.

RECORD_COUNT1.description:
Displays the estimated total row count of the first table. For temporary tables, this
value is always 0.

COUNT1.description: Displays the number of not Null values of the column of the first table.

DISTINCT_COUNT1.description: Displays the number of unique not Null values of the column of the first table.

VALUE_COUNTS1.description:
Displays a comma-separated sorted list of unique column value counts. An incomplete
output of the list is truncated by an asterisk sign. If a
uniform distribution of column values is assumed, then there is
exactly one value count because values are uniformly distributed
and every value counts the same.

VALUE_COUNTS_FREQUENCIES1.description:
Displays a comma-separated list of frequencies of the first table's column values with
the same count. Any frequency refers to that value count with a
corresponding position in the list of value counts. An
incomplete output of the list is truncated by an asterisk sign.
If a uniform distribution of column values is assumed, then
there is exactly one value frequency because values are
uniformly distributed. Generally, this single frequency matches
the distinct count.

MATCHING_VALUES_COUNTS1.description:
Displays a comma-separated list of counts of the first table's column values which find a
join partner. Any matching values count refers to that value
count with corresponding position in the list of value counts.
An incomplete output of the list is truncated by an asterisk
sign. If a uniform distribution of column values is assumed,
then the matching values count the one and only value count that
is calculated from the unified distribution of the value
counts.

MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory size, in bytes, used by the join data statistics object.

M_JOIN_TRANSLATION_TABLES.description: Provides column store join engine translation tables statistics.

COLUMN_NAME1.description: Displays the first table's column name.

PART_ID1.description:
Displays the first table partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the
original table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to
replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is in progress.

VERSION1.description: Displays the version information of the first table.

COLUMN_NAME2.description: Displays the second table's column name.

PART_ID2.description:
Displays the second table partition ID. Returns the
following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the
sequential number of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the
original table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to
replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the
table schema is in progress.

VERSION2.description: Displays the version information of the second table.

IMPLEMENTATION_TYPE_CHANGE_TIME.description:
Displays the last time that the implementation type was
changed.

IMPLEMENTATION_TYPE_CHANGE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of implementation type changes.

VALUE_ID_COUNT1.description:
Displays the number of translated value IDs of the first
table.

VALUE_ID_COUNT2.description:
Displays the number of translated value IDs of the second
table.

TRANSLATION_TABLE_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the memory used by the translation table in bytes.

ESTIMATED_HASHMAP_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the estimated memory used by the HASHMAP implementation in bytes.

ESTIMATED_VECTOR_MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the estimated memory used by the vector implementation in bytes.

LAST_USED_COUNT.description:
Displays the counter to support LRU cache replacement
algorithm.

M_KERNEL_PROFILER.description: Displays the state and provides information about Kernel Profilers in the system. You must have the RESOURCE ADMIN or TRACE ADMIN system privileges to use this view.

APPLICATION_USER_NAME.description: Displays the application user name to filter on.

USER_NAME.description: Displays the SQL user name to filter on.

WAIT_TIME.description:
Deprecated. Displays the wait time between two sampling runs, in
milliseconds (SAMPLING_INTERVAL)

MEMORY_LIMIT.description:
Displays the limit for profiling data in bytes. This is not an exact
limit, a heuristic approach is used to limit memory.

SAMPLING_INTERVAL.description: Displays the sampling interval, in milliseconds.

CONNECTION_ID.description: Displays the connection ID to filter on.

ROOT_STATEMENT_HASH.description: Displays the root statement hash to filter on.

TRACEPROFILE_NAME.description: Displays the user-specific trace profile name to filter on.

START_TIME.description: Displays the time that profiling was started.

STOP_TIME.description: Displays the time that profiling was stopped.

MEMORY_SIZE.description: Displays the current size, in bytes, of the kernel profile.

SAMPLE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of samples stored for the profile.

STATUS.description:
Displays the status of the profiler. Possible values are: STARTED,
STOPPED

M_LANDSCAPE_HOST_CONFIGURATION.description: Specifies the host roles in a distributed landscape.

HOST_ACTIVE.description:
Displays the host active status which is a summary of the active values
of all the services on that host.

HOST_STATUS.description:
Displays the host status:OKThe host is operational; the host actual role equals the
configured role.IGNOREThe host is operational; the host configured as standby is
available, but not used.INFOThe host is operational; the host actual role is different from
the configured role.WARNINGThe host is not operational; the host should become operational
after startup/failover.ERRORThe host not operational; the host is in an erroneous state.

FAILOVER_STATUS.description:
Displays the failover status:(empty value)No failover is pending or active.waiting .. secA failure is detected. Waiting is required to prevent an
unnecessary failover. A restart of another host does not trigger
an immediate failover.waitingA failure is detected. Waiting for another starting host.failover to 'host'Failover to an active host named 'host'.failback to 'host'Failback to worker host active. This happens when a standby host
was assigned and is stopped. There is no automatic failback
while the standby host is assigned because this would cause
downtime.failedThe failover failed. Seenameserver.trcfor
details

FAILOVER_GROUP.description: Deprecated. This has been replaced by the FAILOVER_..._GROUP columns.

FAILOVER_CONFIG_GROUP.description:
Displays the configured failover group.Hosts can be grouped and during failover a host is looked for within the
same group. If no host is available in the same group, hosts from other
groups are used.

NAMESERVER_CONFIG_ROLE.description:
Displays the nameserver configured roles: MASTER/SLAVE. During
installation, up to 3 hosts are automatically configured as MASTER
candidates. All others are SLAVE.

NAMESERVER_ACTUAL_ROLE.description:
Displays the nameserver actual roles: MASTER/SLAVE. Exactly one of the
master candidates is MASTER and all others are SLAVE.The MASTER name server and MASTER index server are both on the same
host.

INDEXSERVER_CONFIG_ROLE.description:
Displays the indexserver configured roles: WORKER/STANDBY.During SAP HANA system installation or host addition, hosts can be
assigned the SAP HANA server roles WORKER or STANDBY. The host can also
be assigned other roles.

INDEXSERVER_ACTUAL_ROLE.description:
Displays the indexserver roles: MASTER, SLAVE, or STANDBY. Exactly one
host is MASTER and all others are SLAVE or STANDBY.

HOST_CONFIG_ROLES.description:
Displays the host's configured database and SAP HANA option roles:
ETS_WORKER, ETS_STANDBY,EXTENDED_STORAGE_WORKER, EXTENDED_STORAGE_STANDBY,STANDBY,
STREAMING, WORKER, XS_WORKER, or XS_STANDBY.

HOST_ACTUAL_ROLES.description:
Displays the host's current database and SAP HANA option roles (these may
change due to host auto-failover): ETS_WORKER, ETS_STANDBY,EXTENDED_STORAGE_WORKER,
EXTENDED_STORAGE_STANDBY,STANDBY, STREAMING, WORKER, XS_WORKER,
or XS_STANDBY.

STORAGE_PARTITION.description:
Deprecated. This has been replaced by the STORAGE_CONFIG_PARTITION, and
STORAGE_ACTUAL_PARTITION columns.

STORAGE_CONFIG_PARTITION.description:
Displays the stable subpath to reassign the same storage partition after
failovers.

STORAGE_ACTUAL_PARTITION.description:
Displays the unique number of the mount sub-directory used by the host
for storing data and logs (also known as the "subpath").

WORKER_CONFIG_GROUPS.description:
Displays the stable classification values to assign hosts to logical
worker groups.

WORKER_ACTUAL_GROUPS.description:
Displays the current classification values to assign hosts to logical
worker groups.

M_LICENSE.description: Provides information on the currently valid license for the SAP HANA database that is installed on this system.

HARDWARE_KEY.description: Displays the hardware key of the SAP HANA installation.

PRODUCT_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the licensed software product, for example, SAP
HANA.

PRODUCT_LIMIT.description:
Displays the licensed amount of main memory as specified by the license
in bytes.

PRODUCT_USAGE.description:
Displays the peak memory allocation since installation, measured hourly,
in bytes.

START_DATE.description: Displays the start date of the validity period of the license.

EXPIRATION_DATE.description: Displays the expiration date of the validity period of the license.

LAST_SUCCESSFUL_CHECK.description:
Displays the latest date on which the license was successfully checked
and found valid.

PERMANENT.description:
Displays TRUE if the license is permanent and FALSE if the license is
temporary.

VALID.description: Indicates whether the license is valid or not: TRUE/FALSE.

ENFORCED.description:
Indicates whether the license is a memory-enforced license or not:
TRUE/FALSE.

LOCKED_DOWN.description:
Indicates whether the system is locked down due to license status:
TRUE/FALSE.

IS_DATABASE_LOCAL.description:
Indicates whether the license is a local database local license:
TRUE/FALSE. If FALSE, then the license is provided globally by the
SystemDB.

USAGE.description:
Displays the database license usage type: NONPRODUCTION, RUNTIME, FULL,
or ADMINISTRATION.

MEASUREMENT_XML.description:
Displays the measurement log produced for this licensed system with the
current memory consumption.

M_LICENSES.description: Provides information on all of the licenses (if any) that are installed on this system.

HARDWARE_KEY.description: Displays the hardware key of the SAP HANA installation.

GLAS_APPLICATION_ID.description: Displays the license ID for the software product.

PRODUCT_NAME.description:
Displays the name of the licensed software product, for example,
SAP HANA.

PRODUCT_DESCRIPTION.description: Displays the description of the licensed software product.

PRODUCT_LIMIT.description:
Displays the allowed product usage as specified by the license in bytes, for example,
main memory.

PRODUCT_LIMIT_DESCRIPTION.description:
Displays the description of the product usage to be measured and
its unit.

PRODUCT_USAGE.description:
Displays the peak product usage value during last 13 months, measured periodically, in
bytes.

MEASUREMENT_INTERVAL.description: Displays the interval of license measurement, in hours.

FIRST_INSTALLATION_TIME.description: Displays the date of the first installation of the license.

START_DATE.description:
Displays the start date of the validity period of the
license.

EXPIRATION_DATE.description:
Displays the expiration date of the validity period of the
license.

LAST_SUCCESSFUL_CHECK.description:
Displays the latest date on which the license was successfully
checked and found valid.

PERMANENT.description:
Displays TRUE if the license is permanent and FALSE if the
license is temporary.

VALID.description: Indicates whether the license is valid or not: TRUE/FALSE.

ENFORCED.description:
Indicates whether the license is a memory-enforced license or
not: TRUE/FALSE.

LOCKED_DOWN.description:
Indicates whether the system is locked down due to license
status: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_DATABASE_LOCAL.description:
Indicates whether the license is a local database local license:
TRUE/FALSE. If FALSE, then the license is provided globally by
the SystemDB.

GLAS_APPLICATION_ID.description: Displays the license ID for the software product.

MEASURE_TIME.description: Displays the the measurement completion time.

VALUE.description: Displays the measured value in bytes. This value is NULLwhen the measurement fails.

SUCCESSFUL.description:
Indicates whether or not the measurement was successful:
TRUE/FALSE.

M_LICENSE_MEASUREMENT_STATISTICS.description: Collects license measurement statistics from tenant databases.

GLAS_APPLICATION_ID.description: Displays the license ID for the software product.

PEAK_VALUE.description: Displays the peak value of the application for the given month and year in bytes.

M_LICENSE_USAGE_HISTORY.description: Information on the maximum resource consumption per time period, used for validity check of license installed on this system (if any).

PRODUCT_USAGE.description:
Displays the maximum utilized amount of main memory used in this period
in bytes.

PERIOD_START_TIME.description: Displays the start time of the period that the maximum was used in.

PERIOD_END_TIME.description: Displays the end time of the period the maximum was used in.

NUM_FAILED_MEASUREMENTS.description: Displays the number of failed measurements.

CLASS_SIZE.description: Displays the object class size in bytes.

OBJECT_TYPE.description: Displays the object type: STD, KEY, or VAR.

KEY_SIZE.description: Displays the object key size in bytes.

KEY_PARTITION_PREFIX_SIZE.description: Displays the key partition prefix length in bytes.

CACHED_KEYS.description: Displays whether the cached keys are active: TRUE/FALSE.

COPY_ON_UPDATE.description: Displays whether copy on update is active: TRUE/FALSE.

PREFETCH.description: Indicates whether the prefetch is active: TRUE/FALSE.

IS_LOADED.description:
Indicates whether the statistics are loaded into the memory:
TRUE/FALSE.

OBJECT_SIZE_SUM.description: Displays the object size sum in bytes.

GC_PENDING_OBJECT_DELETE_COUNT.description:
Displays the count of objects that are deleted and not yet freed by the
garbage collection.

GC_PENDING_OBJECT_DELETE_SIZE_SUM.description:
Displays the transient size sum of the objects that are deleted and not
yet freed by the garbage collection in bytes.

HISTORY_OBJECT_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of transient history object versions that are not yet
freed by the garbage collection.

HISTORY_SIZE_SUM.description:
Displays the size sum, in bytes, of the transient history object versions
that are not yet freed by the garbage collection.

PAGE_SIZE_SUM.description: Displays the page size sum in bytes.

OBJECT_CREATE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of objects created since the last restart.

OBJECT_UPDATE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of objects updated since the last restart.

OBJECT_DELETE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of objects delete since the last restart.

HEAP_USAGE.description: Displays the memory currently used by the container in bytes.

CREATE_TIME.description: Displays the created timestamp.

M_LIVECACHE_CONTAINER_STATISTICS_RESET.description: Provides accumulated LiveCache container statistics since last reset.

M_LIVECACHE_LOCKS.description: Detailed information on the Object Management System (OMS) locks.

ID.description:
Displays the ID of the lock: OID, container ID, or schema ID, depending
on the lock class.

CONTAINER_ID.description: Displays the ID of the corresponding container.

CLASS.description: Displays the class that the lock belongs to.

GRANTED_MODE.description: Displays the mode that the lock has granted to some request(s).

MODE.description:
Displays the mode that the request is requesting on the lock: Free,
IntendShare, IntendExclusive, Share, ShareIntendExclusive, or
Exclusive.

TYPE.description:
Displays when the lock can be removed, either at transaction-end (EOT) or
if not visible anymore (Consistent).

STATE.description: Displays the state of the lock request.

TID.description: Displays the transaction ID belonging to this lock request.

TIMEOUT.description: Displays remaining timeout, if a timeout is specified.

CONTAINER_EXCLUSIVE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of exclusive lock requests on containers.

CONTAINER_SHARED_COUNT.description: Displays the number of shared lock requests on containers.

CONTAINER_COLLISION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of lock collisions on containers.

CONTAINER_TIMEOUT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of lock timeouts on containers.

SCHEMA_EXCLUSIVE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of exclusive lock requests on schemas.

SCHEMA_SHARED_COUNT.description: Displays the number of shared lock requests on schemas.

SCHEMA_COLLISION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of lock collisions on schemas.

SCHEMA_TIMEOUT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of lock timeouts on schemas.

OBJECT_EXCLUSIVE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of exclusive lock requests on objects

OBJECT_SHARED_COUNT.description: Displays the number of shared lock requests on objects.

OBJECT_COLLISION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of lock collisions on objects.

OBJECT_TIMEOUT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of timeouts on objects.

COMMITTED_REQUESTS_COUNT.description: Displays the number of committed requests.

M_LIVECACHE_OMS_VERSIONS.description: Detailed information on the OMS versions that currently exists.

VERSION_ID.description: Displays the ID of the OMS version.

CREATE_DATE.description: Displays the timestamp of the creation of the OMS version.

LAST_OPEN_DATE.description: Displays the timestamp when the OMS version was opened last.

IS_OPEN.description:
Displays the flag indicating whether the version is currently open:
TRUE/FALSE.

CONNECTION_ID.description:
Displays the ID of the connection/session that the version is bound
to.

LIVECACHE_MVCC_SNAPSHOT_TIMESTAMP.description: Displays the consistent view MVCC snapshot timestamp.

HEAP_USAGE.description: Displays the memory that is currently used by the version in bytes.

OBJECT_NAME.description: Displays the object name.

CALL_COUNT.description: Displays the number of calls of the procedure.

SUM_RUN_TIME.description: Displays the total runtime of the procedure in microseconds.

MAX_RUN_TIME.description: Displays the maximum runtime of the procedure in microseconds.

MIN_RUN_TIME.description: Displays the minimum runtime of the procedure in microseconds.

AVERAGE_RUN_TIME.description: Displays the average runtime of the procedure in microseconds.

DEREF_COUNT.description: Displays the number of OID derefs.

DEREF_BASE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of OID derefs against basis.

DEREF_BASE_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of OID derefs against basis from within an OMS
version.

DEREF_KEYED_OBJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of key derefs.

DEREF_KEYED_OBJECT_BASE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of key derefs against basis.

DEREF_KEYED_OBJECT_BASE_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of key derefs against basis from within an OMS
version.

ITER_BASE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of objects read via an OID-iterator from the
basis.

ITER_KEYED_OBJECT_BASE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of objects read via a key-iterator from the
basis.

ITER_BASE_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of objects read via an OID-iterator from the basis
from within an OMS version.

ITER_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of objects, created in an OMS-version, read via an
OID-iterator .

ITER_KEYED_OBJECT_BASE_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of objects read via an OID-iterator from the basis
from within an OMS version.

ITER_KEYED_OBJECT_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of objects, created in an OMS version, read via a
key-iterator.

DEREF_VAR_OBJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of derefs of var objects.

DEREF_VAR_OBJECT_BASE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of derefs of var objects against basis.

DEREF_VAR_OBJECT_BASE_SIZE.description:
Displays the accumulated size of var objects read from the basis in
bytes.

DEREF_VAR_OBJECT_BASE_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of derefs of var objects against basis from within an
OMS version.

DEREF_VAR_OBJECT_BASE_IN_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the accumulated size of var objects read from the basis from
within an OMS version in bytes.

NEW_OBJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of newly created standard objects.

NEW_KEYED_OBJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of newly created keyed objects.

NEW_VAR_OBJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of newly created var objects.

NEW_OBJECT_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of newly created standard objects in an OMS
version.

NEW_KEYED_OBJECT_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of newly created keyed objects in an OMS version.

NEW_VAR_OBJECT_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of newly created var objects in an OMS version.

STORE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of store calls on standard objects.

STORE_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of store calls on standard objects in an OMS
version.

STORE_KEYED_OBJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of store calls on keyed objects.

STORE_KEYED_OBJECT_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of store calls on keyed objects in an OMS
version.

STORE_VAR_OBJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of store calls on var objects.

STORE_VAR_OBJECT_SIZE.description: Displays the accumulated size of store calls on var objects in bytes.

STORE_VAR_OBJECT_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of store calls on var objects in an OMS version.

STORE_VAR_OBJECT_IN_VERSION_SIZE.description:
Displays the accumulated size of store calls on var objects in an OMS
version in bytes.

STORE_BASE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of updates of standard objects in the basis.

STORE_KEYED_OBJECT_BASE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of updates of keyed objects in the basis.

STORE_VAR_OBJECT_BASE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of updates of var objects in the basis.

STORE_VAR_OBJECT_BASE_SIZE.description:
Displays the accumulated sizes of updated objects in the basis in
bytes.

DELETE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of delete calls on standard objects.

DELETE_KEYED_OBJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of delete calls on keyed objects.

DELETE_VAR_OBJECT_COUNT.description: Displays the number of delete calls on var objects.

DELETE_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of delete calls on standard objects in an OMS
version.

DELETE_KEYED_OBJECT_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of delete calls on keyed objects in an OMS
version.

DELETE_VAR_OBJECT_IN_VERSION_COUNT.description: Displays the number of delete calls on var objects in an OMS version.

DELETE_BASE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of deleted standard objects in the basis.

DELETE_KEYED_OBJECT_BASE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of deleted keyed objects in the basis.

DELETE_VAR_OBJECT_BASE_COUNT.description: Displays the number of deleted var objects in the basis.

LOCK_EXCLUSIVE_BASE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of exclusive lock requests on standard objects in the
basis.

LOCK_SHARE_BASE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of share lock requests on standard objects in the
basis.

LOCK_EXCLUSIVE_KEYED_OBJECT_BASE_COUNT.description:
Displays the number of exclusive lock requests on keyed objects in the
basis.

LOCK_SHARE_KEYED_OBJECT_BASE_COUNT.description:

of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table
schema is in progress.

PARTITION_ID.description:
Displays the partition ID. Returns the following:For partitioned tables, the part ID is equal to the sequential number
of the partition, starting at 1.In the case of replicated tables, the part ID is 1 for the original
table and subsequent part IDs are assigned to replica tables.The part ID is 0 for tables that are not partitioned.A part ID value of -1 indicates that a modification of the table
schema is in progress.

SEGMENT_ID.description: Displays the log segment ID within the partition.

FILE_NAME.description: Displays the log segment file name.

FILE_OFFSET.description: Displays the start position of the log segment in the file.

STATE.description:
Displays the log segment state:FormattingThe log segment is being formatted and not yet used.PreallocatedThe log segment has been preallocated, but never used.WritingThe log segment is currently being written.ClosedThe log segment is closed, not backed up and is still required
for restart.TruncatedThe log segment is not required for restart, but has not been
backed up.BackedUpThe log segment has been backed up, but is still required for
restart.RetainedFreeThe log segment has been backed up and is not required for
restart, but is required to resync the system replication
sites.FreeThe log segment has been backed up, it is not required for
restart and can be reused.ClosedIncopleteThe log segment has been closed in an incomplete state on a system replication site. This is a temporary state that
persists until system replication reconnects or a takeover occurs.FreeIncompleteThe log segment has been designated as 'free' in an incomplete state, typically following scenarios like a system
replication takeover or log corruption.

MIN_POSITION.description: Displays the first position contained in the log segment.

MAX_POSITION.description:
Displays the position behind the last log record in the log segment. This
value is for closed log segments only.

HOLE_POSITION.description:
Displays the start position of the log hole before the log segment. This
value is equal to MIN_POSITION if there is no hole.

USED_SIZE.description: Displays the used log segment size in bytes.

TOTAL_SIZE.description: Displays the total log segment size in bytes.

IN_BACKUP.description:
Indicates whether or not the flag for the log segment is in the backup:
TRUE/FALSE

LAST_COMMIT_TIME.description: Displays the timestamp of the last commit in the log segment.

ENCRYPTION_KEY_HASH.description: Displays the hash of the key used for the log segment encryption.

M_LOG_SEGMENTS_RESET.description: Provides log segment statistics since the last reset.

M_MEMORY.description: Deprecated. For service-specific information related to memory, use the M_SERVICE_MEMORY System View topic and for host-specific information related to memory, use the M_HOST_RESOURCE_UTILIZATION System View topic.
